dsl_rule.translation_notes: Translated 'price moved 5% in primary trend direction' using returns > 0.05 for long and < -0.05 for short. Used dc_lower (20-bar Donchian low) as proxy for '20-bar low' exit condition. Higher highs/lower lows pattern captured via high > prev_high and low < prev_low comparisons. Macro regime alignment requires external context not expressible in indicator syntax. Signal is context-dependent, so both entry_long and entry_short are provided with directionally appropriate conditions.

backtest_rule.reason: Translated 'price moved 5% in primary trend direction' using returns > 0.05 for long and < -0.05 for short. Used dc_lower (20-bar Donchian low) as proxy for '20-bar low' exit condition. Higher highs/lower lows pattern captured via high > prev_high and low < prev_low comparisons. Macro regime alignment requires external context not expressible in indicator syntax. Signal is context-dependent, so both entry_long and entry_short are provided with directionally appropriate conditions.

source_rule_text: A trader should maintain positions in markets exhibiting major directional trends and avoid exiting prematurely when early price moves (10% of eventual advance) occur, as trends often extend far beyond initial expectations.
